summary: Tested Babel AST-based preview transform and project creation. All 10 test cases PASSED. The new AST-based Babel plugin in PreviewTab.jsx correctly transforms React/JSX code without SyntaxError. Project creation via modal works without JSON.parse error. Counter component renders correctly in preview.

features_tested.details: Login works via Enter key submission

features_tested.feature: Project creation via modal (no JSON.parse error)
features_tested.details: Created 'BabelTest_8754' project successfully, no error toast

features_tested.feature: Opening existing project 'New Project' with 3 files
features_tested.details: Project hub shows 3 files, navigated to workspace via conversation click

features_tested.feature: Preview tab renders React components via AST Babel transform
features_tested.details: Counter component renders with - button, 0 value, + button
No SyntaxError found in page text
No Compile Error found in page text

features_tested.feature: No red error overlay/banner in preview

features_tested.details: JSX files visible in Code tab
Chat textarea with 'Describe what you want to build...' placeholder found

critical_code_review_comments: The AST-based Babel plugin (__mkPlugin) in PreviewTab.jsx correctly handles import stripping, export default transforms, and named export transforms without regex string replacement - this fixes the SyntaxError issue.
Dashboard.jsx now uses defensive JSON parsing (response.text() + JSON.parse) which prevents JSON.parse errors during project creation.
Login button click still doesn't trigger form submission - users must press Enter key to login (minor UX issue from previous iteration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Babel AST-based preview transform fully tested and working. Key findings: (1) Preview iframe renders Counter component correctly with proper height (862px). (2) Mode label shows 'React (Babel) + Tailwind'. (3) No SyntaxError or Compile Error in preview. (4) Project creation via modal works without JSON.parse error. (5) The 'frontend' supervisor process is FATAL as expected (app uses nextjs_api process on port 3000). (6) Login requires pressing Enter key - clicking Sign In button doesn't work (minor UX issue). (7) Project cards require specific click handler - use JavaScript click with cursor class check.
